seoTitle: Enter Bank Transactions Manually in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to manually enter bank transactions in QuickBooks Online when automatic syncing isn't available, keeping your books current and accurate.
intro: When a bank feed doesn't sync automatically or a transaction slips through, manually entering it keeps your records complete and your reconciliation accurate. This guide walks through how to enter bank transactions manually in QuickBooks Online. It's suited to small business owners and accountants who occasionally need to fill gaps in automated bank syncing.

steps.heading: Identify the missing transaction
steps.body: Compare your bank statement against QuickBooks to spot any transactions that didn't sync automatically.

steps.heading: Open the manual entry option
steps.body: Navigate to the banking or register section in QuickBooks Online to add a transaction by hand.

steps.heading: Enter transaction details
steps.body: Input the date, amount, payee, and category for the transaction, matching the details from your bank statement exactly.

steps.heading: Categorize the transaction accurately
steps.body: Assign it to the correct account so your reports and tax records reflect the transaction properly.

steps.heading: Reconcile against your bank statement
steps.body: Once entered, confirm the transaction matches your bank statement to keep your accounts fully reconciled.

faq.question: Why would a transaction need to be entered manually?
faq.answer: Bank feed delays, connection issues, or cash transactions that don't pass through your bank account can require manual entry.

faq.question: Will manually entered transactions cause duplicates if the feed catches up later?
faq.answer: They can, so it's important to match or exclude the corresponding bank feed transaction once it appears.

faq.question: Can I categorize manually entered transactions the same way as synced ones?
faq.answer: Yes, manual transactions can be categorized and reported on identically to those pulled in automatically.

faq.question: Is manual entry suitable for cash transactions?
faq.answer: Yes, cash transactions that don't appear in your bank feed are a common reason to enter transactions manually.
